message:
br-bd-10pq: compile_error! arch gate documents x86_64+aarch64 support matrix

bd-10pq tracks multi-ISA support. Current reality: x86_64 is primary,
aarch64 is active bring-up, RISC-V is future. Before this commit,
attempting to build on unsupported archs produced cryptic linker /
inline-asm errors deep in the compilation graph.

Add top-of-crate compile_error! in frankenlibc-abi and frankenlibc-core
that fires on target_arch not in {x86_64, aarch64}, with a message
explaining the gap and pointing to bd-10pq as the tracking bead.
Build failures on unsupported archs now fail FAST with actionable
output.

Minimal bead-scoped slice; broader per-ISA inline-asm / intrinsic /
raw-syscall work continues under bd-10pq.

Verified: rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi -p frankenlibc-core
on x86_64 host → exit 0.

files.patch:
@@ -25,6 +25,21 @@
 //! In **hardened** mode, the membrane validates AND applies deterministic healing
 //! (clamp, truncate, quarantine, safe-default) for unsafe patterns.
 
+// Architecture support matrix (bd-10pq). The ABI has inline asm
+// (setjmp_abi.rs global_asm!), x86-specific intrinsics (RTM in
+// htm_fast_path), and raw-syscall sequences that assume a
+// specific ABI register layout. Until each ISA has its own
+// validated code-path, fail at compile time with a clear
+// message rather than silently producing a broken .so.
+#[cfg(not(any(target_arch = "x86_64", target_arch = "aarch64")))]
+compile_error!(
+    "frankenlibc-abi currently supports only target_arch = \"x86_64\" \
+    (primary) and \"aarch64\" (active bring-up). RISC-V and other \
+    ISAs are tracked under bd-10pq — they need per-ISA inline asm \
+    (setjmp_abi), intrinsic replacements (htm_fast_path), and \
+    raw-syscall sequences before this crate can build on them."
+);
+
 #[macro_use]
 mod macros;

@@ -7,6 +7,18 @@
 
 #![deny(unsafe_code)]
 
+// Architecture support (bd-10pq): the `syscall` module gates below
+// on x86_64 / aarch64 because each ISA needs its own validated
+// register layout. Fail fast on other ISAs with a pointer to the
+// tracking bead so build output is actionable.
+#[cfg(not(any(target_arch = "x86_64", target_arch = "aarch64")))]
+compile_error!(
+    "frankenlibc-core currently supports only target_arch = \"x86_64\" \
+    and \"aarch64\". Multi-ISA tracking: bd-10pq. Each new ISA needs \
+    per-arch raw-syscall register conventions in src/syscall/ before \
+    this crate can build on it."
+);
+
 pub mod ctype;
 pub mod dirent;
 pub mod dlfcn;
